findings.evidence: The file says these declarations come from mathlib4#38292/#31449 at lines 17-23, while public declarations use upstream names at lines 44, 58, 81, 147, 267, 275, 281, 305, and 320 (`exists_monotone_partition_Icc`, `Path.codRestrict`, `Path.subpathOn`, quotient `subpathOn_*`, `Path.Homotopic.of_trans_symm`).
findings.fix: Keep the shim names in a TauCeti namespace or otherwise isolate them behind non-conflicting names, then provide local notation/wrappers as needed; remove or migrate them when Mathlib lands the upstream declarations.
findings.issue: The prelude publicly vendors pending upstream declarations under their eventual Mathlib/root names, so a Mathlib bump that adds them will fail with duplicate declarations before downstream imports can use the upstream API.

summary: The PR adds new API without removing existing TauCeti declarations, but its public same-name shim declarations in Mathlib/root namespaces create a predictable Mathlib-bump compatibility break.
